facts: Brian Brobbey scored within the opening 7 minutes for the **Netherlands** — only the 3rd player since 1966 to score with his first three shots at the tournament. *(Men in Blazers)*
**Japan** manager Hajime Moriyasu claims his side is "basically thinking of winning" the entire tournament. *(Men in Blazers)*
**Ecuador** stunned **Germany** 2-1 at MetLife Stadium — Gonzalo Plata's near-post flick off a corner in the 77th minute sent Ecuador to their first knockout appearance in 20 years. *(The Athletic)*
**Curaçao** became the smallest nation ever to qualify for the tournament. *(Men in Blazers)*
Ousmane Dembélé scored a hat-trick in 32 minutes against **Norway** — the second-fastest in tournament history. *(Men in Blazers)*
**Senegal** 5-0 **Iraq** was the biggest-ever win by an African team in tournament history. *(Men in Blazers)*

**Cabo Verde** drew 0-0 with **Saudi Arabia** to secure a knockout berth as tournament debutants. Population: just over 500,000 — and goalkeeper Vozinha gained more Instagram followers during the tournament than Tom Hanks and the Pope. *(Men in Blazers)*

A Brooklyn barbershop conversation overheard strangers debating "Trusty" minutes and Pochettino's uniform choices — proof, the newsletter wrote, that "we live in a proper football nation." *(Men in Blazers)*

**Croatia** described as "growing in confidence with each game, like a dad at a wedding" — Modrić (40) delivered an inch-perfect corner for Vlašić's 83rd-minute winner against **Ghana**. *(Men in Blazers)*
**Colombia** had 24 total shots against **Portugal** — the most attempted in a single tournament match in 60 years — but only 6 on target with an xG of just 1.63. *(Men in Blazers)*
Lionel Messi became the first player in history to score in seven consecutive tournament matches, extending his all-time record to 19 goals. *(ESPN)*
Harry Kane became **England**'s all-time tournament top scorer with 11 goals, surpassing Gary Lineker's record of 10. *(BBC Sport)*
**Panama** became the only team out of all 48 sides to leave the tournament without scoring a single goal. *(BBC Sport)*
Prior to this tournament, **Canada** had lost every single match in their tournament history. Jesse Marsch: "Canadian heroes for the future children of this country." *(Men in Blazers)*
Today's match is the first-ever Round of 32 game in tournament history — the opening match of the expanded knockout format. *(ESPN)*
Both **South Africa** and **Canada** are playing in a knockout match for the first time ever. *(BBC Sport)*
**South Africa** never advanced past the group stage before this tournament, including when they hosted in 2010. *(BBC Sport)*
Multiple **Moroccan** key players — Mazraoui, Amrabat, Salah-Eddine — were born or raised in the **Netherlands**, making their R32 clash "a semifinal and a derby all at once." *(Men in Blazers)*
**Netherlands** vs **Morocco** falls exactly 32 years to the day since the two sides met at USA '94 — also on American soil. Current Dutch coach Ronald Koeman started that match. *(BBC Sport)*
**Brazil** have won 11 of their 14 all-time meetings with **Japan**, but **Japan** stunned them 3-2 in a friendly in October 2025 — their first-ever win over the five-time champions. *(ESPN)*
**Germany**'s 10 group-stage goals came from seven different scorers — the most balanced attack at this tournament. Deniz Undav scored three without starting a single match. *(The Athletic)*

**Paraguay** coach Gustavo Alfaro after shocking **Germany** on penalties: "We dance to the music we're given. Sometimes it's cumbia, sometimes tango, sometimes rumba… today, polka." The Paraguayan government declared a spontaneous national holiday. *(Men in Blazers)*
Antonio Nusa became **Norway**'s youngest-ever tournament goalscorer — nicknamed the "Norwegian Neymar." *(Men in Blazers)*

Haaland has scored 60 international goals in just 53 caps — reaching that mark in 69 fewer appearances than Messi and 77 fewer than Ronaldo. All five of his tournament goals have been one-touch finishes. *(BBC Sport)*
**France** are the first team to score 3+ goals in five straight tournament matches. The Dembélé-Mbappé duo have combined for 6 goals — the most by any pair on record since 1966. *(BBC Sport)*
Gilberto Mora (17 years, 259 days) became the youngest player to start a knockout match since Pelé (17 years, 239 days) in 1958. *(The Athletic)*

Keegan-Michael Key on the **USA**: "There's a violence or savagery about them. The way they're moving… way more intense than I thought they were going to be. There's a fluidity that I have never seen an American team have before." *(Men in Blazers, Keegan-Michael Key)*

Clint Dempsey compared **Bosnia** to the 1990s Charlotte Hornets — "not going to win the whole thing, but on their day they had players who could just go off and hurt you." The **USA** proved him right, holding on with 10 men for 30 minutes. *(Men in Blazers, Dempsey)*

The **USA**'s win over **Bosnia** was only their second-ever knockout victory — the first since 2002 — and the first ever in primetime on home soil. It was also the first time the US has won three games at a single tournament. *(The Athletic, Paul Tenorio)*
Balogun's three tournament goals tie him for second on the all-time US list with Landon Donovan (2010). Only Bert Patenaude's four in 1930 are more. *(The Athletic, Henry Bushnell)*

Kane became the first **England** player to score twice in a tournament knockout match since Lineker vs Cameroon in 1990. He is now joint 6th all-time with 13 tournament goals — level with Pelé. *(BBC Sport, Phil McNulty)*

**Belgium** rallied from 2-0 down against **Senegal** — scoring twice in 159 seconds through Lukaku (87') and Tielemans (89'). Tielemans' 125th-minute penalty is the latest goal in tournament history. *(The Athletic, Tim Spiers)*
Asked to explain **Belgium**'s stunning comeback, Romelu Lukaku kept it simple: "We had a huge pair of balls, and that's what you need in matches like this." *(Men in Blazers, Lukaku)*
Ismaïla Sarr's goal was his 4th in 4 matches — equalling Roger Milla's record for the most goals scored by an African player at a single tournament. *(Men in Blazers, Rog)*
There's a poetic symmetry to the **USA**'s win: the last time they beat a European team was a 1-0 victory over **Bosnia and Herzegovina** back in 2021. *(Men in Blazers, Rog)*

**DR Congo**'s Aaron Wan-Bissaka — born in Croydon and raised in England's youth system before switching to Congo about a year ago — was so good against **England** that Fox commentator Darren Fletcher said if he'd picked a different sport, he'd be the best cornerback in the NFL. *(Men in Blazers, Rog)*

**Austria** are appearing in the Round of 32 at the finals for the first time since 1998 — a 28-year absence from the knockout rounds that ended when Sasa Kalajdzic headed in a 96th-minute equaliser against **Algeria**. *(Sports Mole)*
**Portugal** and **Croatia** have never previously met in a knockout match at the finals, despite both nations being regular tournament fixtures since the 1990s. *(RotoWire)*
**Switzerland** are aiming to reach the Round of 16 for the fourth consecutive time, a feat that would equal their longest-ever streak at this stage of the competition. *(Sports Mole)*

**Algeria** manager Vladimir Petković spent seven years as head coach of **Switzerland** before taking the Algeria job, making Thursday's Round of 32 tie a rare coaching reunion at the finals. *(OneFootball)*

**Spain** have kept clean sheets in eight of their last nine competitive fixtures and are yet to concede a single goal at this tournament — but arrive at the knockouts with all three first-choice wide forwards carrying fitness doubts. *(Fox Sports)*

**Egypt** captain Mohamed Salah has scored 68 goals in 119 international appearances for the Pharaohs, yet this is the first time Egypt have ever reached a knockout match at the finals. — *Fox Sports*

**Cabo Verde** became the smallest nation by population ever to reach the knockout stages of the finals, doing so in their very first tournament appearance — all three of their group games ended in draws. — *ESPN*

**Argentina**'s last defeat in any knockout match at a major tournament came against **Brazil** in the 2019 Copa América semi-finals; Lionel Scaloni's side has not lost a knockout game since that 2-0 defeat in Belo Horizonte. — *Sports Mole*
**Colombia** and **Ghana** have never previously met at the finals — the match in Kansas City will be the first-ever competitive encounter between the two nations on this stage. — *Sports Mole*
**Switzerland** have failed to advance past a Round of 16 at the finals since 1954, a drought of 72 years; their last knockout-stage victory on this stage came in the 1934 tournament. — *Football365*
